name: Community Neighborhood Housing Services, Inc Dba Neighborworks Home Partners
description_short: NeighborWorks Home Partners provides non-profit housing counseling and financial education to help consumers build wealth and achieve homeownership through HUD-approved programs.
description_long:
Community Neighborhood Housing Services, Inc., operating as NeighborWorks Home Partners, is a non-profit organization dedicated to expanding economic opportunity through housing and financial counseling services. The organization is part of the national NeighborWorks network, which has served millions of households since its inception. Their mission centers on helping individuals and families build financial stability and achieve homeownership goals through professional guidance and education.

NeighborWorks Home Partners offers HUD-approved housing counseling, financial literacy education, and homeownership preparation programs. Their services are designed to address barriers to wealth-building and housing access, particularly for underserved communities. They provide personalized counseling on credit improvement, down payment assistance navigation, budgeting, and mortgage readiness. The organization operates as a legitimate non-profit with certification and oversight from HUD and other regulatory bodies.

What distinguishes NeighborWorks Home Partners is their integration into a national network of housing organizations with decades of collective experience. They combine expert counseling with community-focused programming and educational resources. As a HUD-approved counseling agency, their services meet federal standards for quality and effectiveness. Their non-profit status means counselors prioritize consumer benefit over sales commissions, unlike for-profit credit repair or lending companies.

A significant limitation is that the organization's website is currently inaccessible (returning a 403 Forbidden error), which prevents detailed verification of current service offerings, locations, pricing, and specific program availability. Consumers cannot independently confirm program details, counselor credentials, or success metrics through the website. This creates friction for individuals trying to research the organization before engaging with services. Interested parties would need to contact the organization directly by phone or mail to obtain detailed information.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselor designation ensures federal compliance and credibility
Non-profit structure means no hidden fees or sales commissions driving recommendations
Part of established national NeighborWorks network with multi-decade track record
Focuses on root causes of financial problems rather than quick fixes
Services address homeownership barriers for underserved populations
cons: Website currently inaccessible (403 Forbidden error), limiting ability to verify services and programs
Cannot confirm current service areas, locations, or availability of specific programs
No online pricing or program details visible due to website access issues
Limited ability to research counselor credentials and client testimonials
May have wait times typical of non-profit organizations with limited staff
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking HUD-approved mortgage readiness counseling
Individuals with credit challenges wanting non-profit financial guidance without pressure
Communities seeking legitimate housing and financial literacy education programs
diagnosis: NeighborWorks Home Partners is best for consumers seeking free or low-cost, non-profit housing and financial counseling from a HUD-certified organization. The primary caveat is that their website is currently inaccessible, so direct contact is necessary to confirm current services, locations, and program availability.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 533 Dale St N, Saint Paul, MN 55103
meta_description: NeighborWorks Home Partners provides housing counseling and financial education for homeownership through HUD-approved programs.
